Cupr..Back: In the lower part of the chest, in the region of the xiphoid appendix, there is a spasmodic condition that is very troublesome. It seems to be at times a constriction so severe that he thinks he will die, and at others a feeling as if he were transfixed with a knife from the xiphoid appendix to the back.
Cupr..Back, pain: a feeling as if he were transfixed with a knife from the xiphoid appendix to the back.
Cupr..Chest: Cramping of the muscles of the chest; cramping of the calves; cramping all over. Spasms of the chest; spasms of the larynx; spasms of the whole respiratory system of such a character that the child seems to be choking to death. Constriction across the chest, suffocation, cramps of the legs.
Cupr..Chill: the extremities are cold, there is jerking of the muscles, cramping of the extremities and of the fingers and toes, spasms of the chest; he is cold, mottled, blue in blotches, going into collapse; the finger nails and toe nails and the hands and feet are blue.
Cupr..Extremities: In the fingers and toes and in the extremities the spasmodic condition increases and extends until the limbs are in a state of great exhaustion. Tonic contractions, the limbs being drawn up with great violence and it seems as if the frame would be torn to pieces by the violent contractions of the muscles everywhere.
Cupr..Extremities, coldness: the extremities are cold, there is jerking of the muscles, cramping of the extremities and of the fingers and toes, spasms of the chest; he is cold, mottled, blue in blotches, going into collapse; the finger nails and toe nails and the hands and feet are blue.
Cupr..Generalities: Convulsive cramps all over the body with twitching, jerking, trembling and blueness of the skin. Everything he does, all his actions are spasmodic, are convulsive. All the sphincters are convulsive, All the activities are irregular, disorderly and convulsive when poisoned with copper.
Cupr..Genitalia female: In spasmodic conditions that come on during menstruation Cuprum is also useful. Painful menstruation with spasms commencing in the fingers and extending over the body. Tonic contractions that look like hysterical manifestations. Violent dysmenorrhoea with delirium, turning up of the eyes, contortions of the face and epileptiform manifestations.
Cupr..Genitalia female, pain: Before or during menses, or after suppression, violent, unbearable cramps in the abdomen. Painful menstruation with spasms commencing in the fingers and extending over the body.
Cupr..Head: Violent congestion in the head, violent pains in the head. Tingling pain in the vertex, severe pain in the vertex, bruised pain. Crawling sensation in vertex, stitches in the temples. Congestion of the brain, Meningitis. Headache after epileptic attacks.
Cupr..Mind: Cuprum has many mental symptoms. It has a great variety in its delirium, incoherent prattling, talking of all sorts of subjects incoherently. It has produced a variety of mental symptoms; delirium, incoherency of speech, loss of memory.
Cupr..Mind, restlessness, nervousness: In the Cuprum patient the nerves are all the time wrought up to the highest tension; wants to fly, wants to do something dreadful. Impulsiveness. Compelled to do something; restless and tossing about a constant uneasiness; nervous trembling; always tired.
Cupr..Mouth: Inflammation of the tongue. Paralysis of the tongue, It is not an uncommon thing to find paralysis in Cuprum after convulsions, The violence of the convulsions seems to have brought about a reaction and paralytic weakness, a numbness and tingling, a loss of motility.
Cupr..Rectum: In cholera morbus with gushing, watery stools and copious vomiting, the stomach and bowels are emptied of their contents. The patient is fairly emptied out.
Cupr..Respiration: Whenever the respiratory organs are affected there is spasmodic breathing, dyspnoea. There is also rattling in the chest. The more dyspnoea there is the more likely his thumbs will be clenched and the fingers cramped.
Cupr..Respiration, difficult: Spasmodic respiration; great dyspnea, asthmatic breathing. Attacks of spasmodic asthma and most violent spasmodic coughs. He is not able to take a full breath.
Cupr..Sleep: Twitching and jerking and starting during sleep. Grinding of the teeth with brain affections.
Cupr..Sleep, restless: Twitching and jerking and starting during sleep.
Cupr..Stomach: Spasms of the stomach. In the stomach and bowels periodical cramps. Cramps coming periodically. It has pain in the stomach, and a pain under the xiphoid appendix that seems as if it would take his life.
Cupr..Stomach, eructations: Some say it feels as if a lump were in that region and others as if much wind were collected in the stomach.
Cupr..Stomach, pain: Pressure in the stomach. It has pain in the stomach, and a pain under the xiphoid appendix that seems as if it would take his life. If it is not removed he will certainly die in a little while.
Cupr..Abdomen, pain: In young girls beginning to menstruate, violent cramps in the limbs, cramps in the abdomen, diarrhoea, cramps in the uterus. Before or during menses, or after suppression, violent, unbearable cramps in the abdomen.
Cupr..Bladder, urination: Convulsions with suppressed or scanty urine. No urine in the bladder.
Cupr..Generalities, pain: He is full of cramps and is compelled to cry out with the pain from the contractions of the muscles.
Cupr..Generalities, weakness: Great weakness of the muscles, and relaxation of the body when the convulsions are not on.
Cupr..Head, congestion: Violent congestion in the head, violent pains in the head. Congestion of the brain, Meningitis. Instead of convulsions it may take the form of congestion of the brain with violent delirium.
Cupr..Mind, restlessness: Compelled to do something; restless and tossing about a constant uneasiness; nervous trembling; always tired.
Cupr..Appetite: Great thirst for cold drinks. Desire for warm food and drinks. Eats hastily. Indigestion from milk.
